Nature's Melody  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Charges: Unlimited
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 19
DMG: 15 Dmg Bonus: 35 AC: 20
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +10 STA: +15 CHA: +10 AGI: +5 HP: +100
SV FIRE: +13 SV COLD: +13 SV MAGIC: +13
Effect: Nature's Melody (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 3.0)
WT: 1.0 Size: TINY
Class: BRD
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Nature's Melody
Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Charges: Unlimited
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 19
DMG: 15 Dmg Bonus: 35 AC: 20
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +10 STA: +15 CHA: +10 AGI: +5 HP: +100
SV FIRE: +13 SV COLD: +13 SV MAGIC: +13
Effect: Nature's Melody (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 3.0)
WT: 1.0 Size: TINY
Class: BRD
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Type:1H Piercing
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:rethcaw
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-20 23:03:49
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Ultra Rare
Level to Attain: 60
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Plane of Growth
NPC Name
Tunare


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Plane of Growth


Uploaded September 4th, 2017 by Nniki
Send a correction
Post Comment
Nice effect clickable from inventory
QuoteReply
# Mar 27 2005 at 10:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757700860
Scholar
49 posts
I got this tonight since I don't have a Songblade or Rapier of Somber Notes yet and it's nice. Nature's Melody is clickable from inventory. Killed Tunare with 2 clerics, 2 warriors, bard, enchanter, 2 rogues all in about GoD Qvic gear or better. Wasn't too bad since she's perma rooted. Once most of the zone was cleared, just agroed Tunare and had pet keep her busy while we cleared the remaining mobs in the zone that she summoned. Was much easier than pulling them.

good weapon

I let my other bard friend have this weapon because he didnt yet have epic or any other uber weapon. My EQ days are about over and I knew he'd get more use out of it then if I had it. Its a great weapon. Gratz laskar.

# Feb 26 2003 at 1:20 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759368833
70 posts
hehe thats somthing i can support to, everyone starts somewhere, all the uber people were using the same newbie tunic when they started. i must say that when i started my ranger i never htought epic would be anywhere near possible, now i have epic, and also have better, i am very very proud to say now that i am one of the few rangers in fennin who has and doesnt use swiftwind, so just goes to show, that u can get what u want u just have to work at it =P

# Jan 11 2003 at 10:06 PM Rating: Good

I started my bard on EverQuest on February 18, 2002. Today is January 11, 2003. When I first saw a Nature's Melody on Thott's profile, I thought it was the best thing in the world and told all my RL friends I wanted one so bad and that I would get one some day. They all said to dream on. Well, I got one tonight =). All you people who say "I WANT THIS ITEM!" and people say you'll never get it, just keep trying, and you will =).

Follower Leader. 65 Cheerleader. <Sphaera>
Mithanial Marr Server.

QuoteReply
# Nov 11 2002 at 2:26 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591772474665
18 posts
This and Siren Song is a great combo. I passed a roll on Siren Song a couple nights ago because I have this in primary and epic in secondary.

The real killer on the epic is that it sucks compared to this and Siren Song... except the very nice instrument modifier on it.

Bard songs are far more important than their melee. Don't see myself banking the epic for a while... at least until a PoP item can modify all songs.

__DEL__1591775428068
11 posts
If the proc was changed to Nature's Melody, does it still have the same effect ?
▲ Page top
RE: Proc
QuoteReply
# Aug 11 2002 at 9:43 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591780808232
28 posts
Nature's Melody is actually better than SotDS. 5% more haste, 5 more ATK, etc. as per Magelo. Imagine duel wield this and Siren Song, and get them both to proc...then maybe toss in the epic and get that to proc...as far as I know, they all stack and the haste will be maxxed but your still getting ATK and AC from them =)
▲ Page top
RE: Proc
QuoteReply
# Oct 24 2002 at 2:39 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592758900088
Scholar
63 posts
Haste stacking....a proc/right click is the same as a song or a spell. There is also permanent (while equipped) item haste, as in the FBSS (flowing black silk sash).

You may have only one standard song/spell, one item haste, and one special bard stacking haste sone (Vah Shir or Ervaj line). (see exceptions at the web page listed below)

So, if you have enchanter haste for 55%, have an FBSS for 21% and bard is singing VS Battlecry for 15%. You have 91% haste. If the weapon procs (or is right clicked for 60%) the 55% chanter haste is replaced with 60% and you have 96% haste. If the bard's epic then procs (55%) haste, you get all the other epic buffs but no additional haste because you already have spell/song haste over the 55%. Haste stays the same. Typically the higher haste takes effect.

See http://cloud.prohosting.com/~talone/HasteData.htm

The bard Vah Shir line will raise haste cap. For instance, if you have 70% chanter haste and are wearing the Cloak of Flames for 36% and your characters cap is 100%. You only have one hundred %haste because of the cap. If the bard sings Battlecry of VS, a 15% song. Your haste goes from your 100% cap to 115%. (no you don't get back the 5% of 'lost' item haste).

Bard Ervaj Stacks but does not raise the cap.

There are some that argue that Vah Shir line does not add haste, only raises the cap by the 15% (or 25% for Warsong of VS). About that I do not know.

Disclaimer: So it was explained to me, could all be bs.
▲ Page top
RE: Proc
QuoteReply
# Oct 16 2002 at 10:04 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591775299012
45 posts
Nature's Melody is a right-click effect with a 3 sec cast time.

▲ Page top
Song of the Deep Sea's
QuoteReply
# Jan 01 2002 at 10:50 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361473653
Scholar
34 posts
Got this just the other night. Song of the Deep Sea's isn't a permanent effect, its a proc.

Song of the Deep Sea's
Increase Agility by 30
Increase Attack Speed by 60%
Increase Armor Class (AC) by 15
Increase Strength by 30
Allowable Targets: group
Spell Duration: 25 ticks (2.5 minutes)

Stacks with Bard Epic, but causes a twisting problem because the proc has a short spell cast time.
